Class HistoryquoteLegacyImport

java.lang.Object
grafioschtrader.priceupdate.historyquote.HistoryquoteLegacyImport
public class HistoryquoteLegacyImport extends Object
Round-trip counterpart of the legacy view's CSV export: imports rows into historyquote_legacy from a CSV file produced by the legacy view (or hand-written with the same column shape).

Expected CSV (first line is the header, separator is ;, columns may be in any order; case-insensitive matching): 

  date;transferDate;close;open;high;low;volume
  2020-01-15;2024-03-10;150.25;148.50;151.00;148.00;1000000
  2020-01-16;2024-03-10;152.00;150.50;153.00;150.00;1200000

Field semantics:

  • date (REQUIRED) — the trading date the quote belongs to.
  • close (REQUIRED) — the closing price.
  • open, high, low, volume — optional; missing values are stored as NULL.
  • transferDate — the archival batch date; defaults to LocalDate.now() when the column is absent or the cell is blank. Determines the post-archival split factor applied at supplement time.

Provenance: imported rows always land in legacy with create_type = MANUAL_IMPORTED. When later supplemented back into live, that origin label is preserved (per insertLegacyIntoLive).

Conflict handling: insertion is INSERT IGNORE on the unique key (id_securitycurrency, date). A row already present in the shadow keeps its existing transfer_date and create_type; the importer counts that case as notOverridden.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static final class 
    HistoryquoteLegacyImport.LegacyImportRow
    Mutable JavaBean used as the target of ValueFormatConverter.convertAndSetValue(Object, String, String, Class), which delegates to Apache Commons PropertyUtils.setSimpleProperty — that helper resolves properties via JavaBean introspection (getters/setters), NOT via public fields, so the bean must expose proper accessors.
